definition: Private credit ($1.8T) is structurally obscuring the true credit cycle position through PIK toggles, extend-and-amend capacity, and mark-to-model valuations that absorb distress historically visible in public markets. The directional concern is valid but the magnitude of the true default rate gap is unknowable (could be 20% or 60%) given opacity.
evidence: PIK toggles and extend-and-amend capacity absorb distress
150-250bp true default rate gap partially refuted as unfalsifiable
open_questions: What disclosure improvements would be needed to assess true credit cycle position?
Could a private credit repricing event spill into public markets despite structural separation?
